speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been feeling a bit off lately—mostly tired and a little dizzy when I stand up.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for coming in. Let’s start with your vitals—I just checked your blood pressure: systolic is 137 mmHg.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re currently on Lisinopril 20mg once daily for your hypertension—we’ll keep that in mind as we review today.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve been taking it consistently, though I did miss a dose two days ago because I forgot.

speaker: doctor
text: I also ran a random blood glucose test—it came back at 10.9 mmol/L, which is elevated.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your history and exam, your primary diagnosis remains Essential Hypertension.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure today is 105 mmHg, and your heart rate is steady at 70 bpm.

speaker: patient
text: 105? That seems high—I thought my numbers were improving.

speaker: doctor
text: It is elevated, and we’ll address that—but first, any new symptoms since your last visit?

speaker: patient
text: Yes—I’ve had blurred vision over the past few days, especially in the mornings.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, I just rechecked your blood pressure—it’s now 112 mmHg systolic, so there’s some variability; let’s monitor closely.

speaker: patient
text: That’s a big drop from the first reading. Could the high glucose be affecting my eyes?

speaker: doctor
text: Very likely—blurred vision plus a glucose of 10.9 suggests possible hyperglycemia-related osmotic changes in the lens. We need to assess for diabetes.

speaker: patient
text: I didn’t know my sugar was that high—I haven’t been checking regularly at home.

speaker: doctor
text: Given this, along with your hypertension and symptoms, we should initiate treatment for hyperglycemia while continuing BP management.

speaker: patient
text: Okay—what do you recommend?

speaker: doctor
text: I’m going to switch you to Metformin 500mg twice daily, starting today. We’ll hold off on adjusting Lisinopril for now but monitor both closely.

speaker: patient
text: Got it. Should I watch for side effects like stomach upset?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes—start with one dose with dinner tonight to minimize GI effects, then add the morning dose tomorrow. Also, continue checking your BP at home daily.

speaker: patient
text: Just checked again before leaving—the diastolic is still 105 mmHg, same as that number you mentioned earlier.
